title: Tangy Juicy Turkish Rice Paper Rolls
description: Delight in these energizing Turkish rice paper rolls, filled with crunchy vegetables and zesty spices, perfect for a satvik-friendly meal.
instructions: Soak the rice paper sheets in warm water for about 5 minutes, until they become pliable and easy to handle.
Prepare the filling by finely chopping the onion, garlic, carrot, cucumber, and tomato, and saut�3e them in a pan with sesame oil until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the minced lamb to the pan and cook until it is browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the cumin, black pepper, salt, lemon juice, and honey to the pan and stir to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
To assemble the rolls, lay a rice paper sheet flat on a clean surface and place a tablespoon of the filling in the center of the sheet.
Fold the bottom edge of the sheet up over the filling, and then fold in the sides and roll the sheet up to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining rice paper sheets and filling.
Heat the ttukbaegi cooking liquid in the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat until it is hot and bubbly.
Add the rolls to the pot and cook for about 5 minutes, until they are lightly browned and crispy.
Serve the rolls hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley and mint, and sesame seeds if desired.
